channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-10320] sanity test 17g fails with ‘FAIL: <machine-name>:LBUG/LASSERT detected’.
item.description:
sanity test 17g is failing when run with Ubuntu Lustre client with 

 ‘FAIL: <machine-name>:LBUG/LASSERT detected’

In particular, check_node_health found

rcmd: xpoll: protocol failure in circuit setup

From the test_log, the output from test 17g is

== sanity test 17g: symlinks: really long symlink name and inode boundaries ========================== 19:15:57 (1511378157)
CMD: onyx-33vm12 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n version 2>/dev/null ||
				/usr/sbin/lctl lustre_build_version 2>/dev/null ||
				/usr/sbin/lctl --version 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2
CMD: onyx-33vm12 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n version 2>/dev/null ||
				/usr/sbin/lctl lustre_build_version 2>/dev/null ||
				/usr/sbin/lctl --version 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2
CMD: onyx-33vm12 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n version 2>/dev/null ||
				/usr/sbin/lctl lustre_build_version 2>/dev/null ||
				/usr/sbin/lctl --version 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2
xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx


Resetting fail_loc on all nodes...CMD: onyx-33vm10,onyx-33vm11,onyx-33vm12,onyx-33vm9.onyx.hpdd.intel.com lctl set_param -n fail_loc=0 	    fail_val=0 2>/dev/null
done.
19:15:59 (1511378159) waiting for onyx-33vm10 network 5 secs ...
19:15:59 (1511378159) network interface is UP
CMD: onyx-33vm10 rc=0;
			val=\$(/usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n catastrophe 2>&1);
			if [[ \$? -eq 0 && \$val -ne 0 ]]; then
				echo \$(hostname -s): \$val;
				rc=\$val;
			fi;
			exit \$rc
pdsh@onyx-33vm9: onyx-33vm10: rcmd: xpoll: protocol failure in circuit setup
sanity test_17g: @@@@@@ FAIL: onyx-33vm10:LBUG/LASSERT detected

Looking at the console logs for the node that has the error provides no information; the log may have been overwritten.

We see these errors when check_node_health() finds an issue

6312 check_node_health() {
6313         local nodes=${1:-$(comma_list $(nodes_list))}
6314 
6315         for node in ${nodes//,/ }; do
6316                 check_network "$node" 5
6317                 if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
6318                         do_node $node "rc=0;
6319                         val=\\\$($LCTL get_param -n catastrophe 2>&1);
6320                         if [[ \\\$? -eq 0 && \\\$val -ne 0 ]]; then
6321                                 echo \\\$(hostname -s): \\\$val;
6322                                 rc=\\\$val;
6323                         fi;
6324                         exit \\\$rc" || error "$node:LBUG/LASSERT detected"
6325                 fi
6326         done
6327 }

We have only seen this issue with Ubuntu clients and started on October 26, 2017. Logs for some of these failures are at
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/7f763f4e-d76b-11e7-a066-52540065bddc
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/bbeaa6be-d459-11e7-9c63-52540065bddc
https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/0b504084-d42c-11e7-a066-52540065bddc

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
This has been fixed in the lab, not a LU bug

https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/b20d9586-e924-11e7-9840-52540065bddc
Please try using xinted instead of inetd and see if this goes away.

There's something wrong with the inetd configuration. From /scratch/logs/syslog/trevis-18vm2.log-20171203.gz while sanity test 17g was running:

Dec 1 19:29:42 trevis-18vm2 inetd[524]: shell/tcp server failing (looping), service terminated for 10 min
